crime.value: Child murder under bridge; knife left at scene but killer wore gloves making fingerprinting useless
crime.quote: A young child was murdered under the bridge and his body was left to float upon the water.

victim.value: Geraldo, not even six years old, nephew of narrator Jim; killed by Lewis Mackson in revenge for affair
victim.quote: The child was not even six years old... his name was Geraldo and he was not even six yet.

culprit_and_motive.value: Lewis Mackson killed Geraldo in revenge after discovering Mike's affair with his wife; Mike then kills Mackson
culprit_and_motive.quote: The man I wronged was Lewis Mackson... He took it into his own hands to kill my child and attack me, so I killed him.

resolution.value: Mike confesses guilt in court and explains the chain of revenge to provide closure for Mackson's daughter
resolution.quote: For the murder of Lewis J. Mackson, how do you plead? Guilty.

investigator.value: Jim, the narrator and brother of victim's father Mike; witnesses original crime and becomes accomplice to revenge
investigator.quote: Yesterday I witnessed the unspeakable... his father was my brother.

suspect_architecture.value: Limited suspect pool: Mike (the vengeful father/perpetrator), Lewis Mackson (the original child killer/victim), unnamed bar patron (minor accomplice). Story reveals Mike as both suspect and confessor.
suspect_architecture.quote: For the murder of Lewis J. Mackson, how do you plead? Guilty.

red_herring.value: Mike's initial claim about seeking revenge for his son's murder misdirects from the revealed adultery motive - Mackson killed Mike's child born from an affair.
red_herring.quote: I cheated on my newlywed wife and also became the reason why a woman cheated on her husband

clue_fairness.value: Reader learns alongside narrator Jim about Mike's revenge plan, the bar distraction scheme, and courtroom revelation of the affair backstory.
clue_fairness.quote: I just said I thought it would be funny if he distracted the cop and that if he did I'd give him a twenty

social_world.value: Family dynamics between estranged brothers, marital infidelity networks, and legal/police institutional authority during investigation and trial.
social_world.quote: I'd probably be too loud for his wife

reveal_mechanic.value: Courtroom confession reveals the true motive - adultery and retribution rather than random child murder - through Mike's final statement.
reveal_mechanic.quote: He took it into his own hands to kill my child and attack me, so I killed him

media_and_audience.value: Legal proceedings as public theater, with Mike's statement directed at his daughter in the courtroom audience rather than for legal defense.
media_and_audience.quote: it could make his daughter understand why her second father wouldn't come home. That was worth it.

investigator_wound.value: Narrator Jim's guilt over family estrangement and complicity in Mike's revenge plot, torn between loyalty and moral responsibility.
investigator_wound.quote: I knew it was wrong but I ran out of there with Mike
